rationale: Rotation changes the orientation of the shoot relative to gravity and is hypothesised to promote amyloplast displacement.
Endodermal statocyte gravity sensing provides the cellular and tissue context in which amyloplast displacement is interpreted.
Amyloplast displacement is hypothesised to feed into LAZY/LZY-dependent gravity signalling.
The LAZY/LZY module is hypothesised to influence PIN-mediated auxin transport polarity.
PIN-mediated redistribution is hypothesised to generate auxin asymmetry across the shoot.
Auxin asymmetry is hypothesised to promote differential cell expansion.
Unequal cell expansion is expected to produce curvature.
Curvature contributes to the time-dependent bending trajectory measured after gravistimulation.
Genetic background may alter the strength, timing, or form of LAZY/LZY-related signalling without being represented as a simple activation or inhibition.
Genetic background may alter auxin transport or redistribution without a known simple positive or negative sign.
Genetic background may alter growth capacity or responsiveness without a known simple positive or negative sign.
Genetic background may alter the observed bending trajectory over time.
Observed bending behaviour can be used to classify families or genotypes as gravitropic or agravitropic.
